summary: c=2 native batch full-attention launch-shape experiment: 1024-thread small-row launch improves traced context-kernel time and modestly improves unprofiled native c=2 decode, but retained c2 promotion remains blocked by c1 scaling.

software.hipcc_version:
HIP version: 7.2.53211-364a905
AMD clang version 22.0.0git (/srcdest/rocm-llvm f58b06dce1f9c15707c5f808fd002e18c2accf7e)
Target: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
Thread model: posix
InstalledDir: /opt/rocm/lib/llvm/bin

code_change.description: hipengine_qwen35_paged_full_attn_decode_context_bf16_batch_spans uses 1024 threads for rows<=2 and max_context_len<1024; c>=4 rows keep 256 threads. Shared-memory warp buffer allocation follows the selected launch shape.

conclusion.reason: 1024-thread native batch full-attention launch improves the traced context kernel to 0.824x of prior and unprofiled projection-check aggregate decode to 1.026x of prior, but c2 aggregate remains only 0.796x c1, so no retained c2 performance claim is made.

commands.primary_verify: active loop verify command after commit 5a06c0e
commands.profiled: rocprofv3 --kernel-trace --output-format csv -d /tmp/hipengine-e2e-native-c2-launch1024-profile-clean-iter109 -- env HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py ... --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-native-c2-launch1024-profiled-clean-iter109.json
